source.definition: a unit of time equal to sixty seconds

explanation.sourceText: The word 'minute' comes from the Latin phrase 'minuta pars' meaning 'small part', referring to the division of an hour into smaller parts.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul „minut“ provine din latinescul 'minuta pars' (parte mică), desemnând subdiviziunea orei în unități mai mici.

source.additional: A minute consists of 60 seconds and is an important measure of time in daily life.

target.definition: o perioadă de șaizeci de secunde

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word 'minut' derives from the Latin 'minutum', the neuter participle of the verb 'minuere' (to lessen), referring to a small unit of time.
explanation.targetText: Das rumänische Wort „minut“ kommt vom lateinischen 'minutum', dem Partizip Neutrum von 'minuere' (verringern), und bezeichnet eine kleine Zeiteinheit.

target.additional: „Minut“ wird in der Zeitmessung verwendet und ist überall bekannt.

explanation.sourceText: A time indication in hours and minutes.

examples.sourceText: The clock strikes every full minute.
examples.targetText: Ceasul bate la fiecare minut întreg.

explanation.sourceText: Also used to describe a short span of time.
explanation.targetText: Este folosit și pentru a descrie o perioadă scurtă de timp.

examples.targetText: Durata este de doar un minut.

explanation.sourceText: The word is used for the time unit of sixty seconds.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul este folosit pentru unitatea de timp de șaizeci de secunde.

examples.targetText: Un minut are șaizeci de secunde.

examples.sourceText: We only have five minutes left until departure.
examples.targetText: Mai avem doar cinci minute până la plecare.

examples.sourceText: I count every minute until the end of work.
examples.targetText: Număr fiecare minut până la sfârșitul programului.

examples.sourceText: The clock strikes every full minute.
examples.targetText: Ceasul bate la fiecare minut întreg.

learningTips.text: The Romanian word 'minut' sounds very similar to the English 'minute', making it easy to remember as both refer to the same time unit.
Think of 'minut' ending with '-ut' like 'cut'—a small cut of time, just like a minute is a small part of an hour.
Imagine a clock ticking every minute; 'minut' sounds like 'minute' and reminds you of the quick passing of time.
When you hear 'minute', connect it to 'minuere', the Latin root meaning 'to lessen', which explains why a minute is a small unit of time.
